name: AccordeonCard
description: A collapsible accordion card component used as a layout element in dynamic forms. Renders a header with a toggle chevron and an animated body section that expands or collapses. Supports custom header actions, always-open mode, and configurable title sizes.
when_to_use: When grouping form fields inside a collapsible card with a visible header
When embedding form sections that can be toggled open or closed by the user
When using the 'accordeon_card' layout type in dynamicConfig
avoid: Do not use directly as an input component — it is a layout wrapper
Avoid when a non-collapsible section is preferred; use Card or Section instead

props.name: children
props.description: Content rendered inside the accordion body.

props.name: name
props.description: Identifier used for QA attributes on the toggle button.

props.name: header
props.description: Content displayed in the accordion header.

props.name: alwaysOpen
props.description: When true the body is always expanded and the toggle button is hidden.

props.name: className
props.description: Additional CSS class for the root element.

props.name: classNameBody
props.description: Additional CSS class for the body element.

props.name: description
props.description: Optional description rendered as HTML inside the header.

props.name: headerActionsTemplate
props.description: Extra content rendered in the header actions area alongside the toggle button.

props.name: ignoreHeaderToggle
props.description: When true clicking the header does not toggle the body.

props.name: onToggle
props.description: Callback fired when the open state changes.

props.name: open
props.description: Controlled open state of the accordion.

props.name: titleSize
props.description: Size of the title text; defaults to 'm'.

name: ArrayBase
description: A built-in array input component that renders each item using the spec's item sub-spec and an add-item button. Supports vertical or right-aligned add button positioning and optional item prefix labels between items. Used as the 'base' array input type in dynamicConfig.
when_to_use: When rendering an array of repeated form fields specified by ArraySpec
When the viewSpec.type is 'base' for an array field
avoid: Do not use when a tabular layout is needed — use TableArrayInput instead
Do not use for multi-select from an enum — use MultiSelect instead

name: Card
description: A collapsible card with a header, optional title, description tooltip, and action slot. Built on top of GravityUI Card and Popover. Supports always-open mode, disabling header toggle, and empty-body detection to auto-hide the card border.
when_to_use: When displaying a titled, collapsible container for a group of form fields
When used as the layout component inside CardOneOf
avoid: Do not use as an input — it is a layout/container component
Avoid when a simple non-card section is needed — use Section or Row instead

props.name: children
props.description: Content rendered inside the card body.

props.name: name
props.description: Identifier used for QA attributes.

props.name: actions
props.description: Action elements rendered in the header right area.

props.name: alwaysOpen
props.description: When true the card is permanently expanded.

props.name: checkEmptyBody
props.description: When true detects an empty body and applies empty-body styling.

props.name: description
props.description: HTML description rendered via HelpMark tooltip.

props.name: disableHeaderToggle
props.description: Prevents clicking the header from toggling the body.

props.name: onToggle
props.description: Callback fired when the open state changes.

props.name: open
props.description: Controlled open state.

props.name: title
props.description: Title content for the card header; string titles receive ellipsis truncation and a tooltip.

name: CardOneOf
description: An object input component that combines the useOneOf toggler with a Card layout. Used for oneOf fields that need a card container with a header toggler and optional remove button when inside an array. Automatically opens when a validation error occurs inside the card body.
when_to_use: When the viewSpec.type is 'card_oneof' for an object field
When a oneOf field should be displayed inside a collapsible card
avoid: Do not use when a flat or inline oneOf is preferred — use OneOf or OneOfFlat instead

name: Checkbox
description: A boolean input component that wraps GravityUI Checkbox. Registered as the 'base' boolean input type in dynamicConfig. Reads and writes boolean values via the dynamic-forms field integration.
when_to_use: When the viewSpec.type is 'base' for a boolean field
When a standard checkbox is needed for a true/false field
avoid: Do not use for toggle-style UI — use Switch instead
Do not use for multi-select from an array — use CheckboxGroup instead

name: CheckboxGroup
description: An array input component that renders a group of checkboxes derived from the spec's enum values. Supports horizontal or vertical placement controlled by viewSpec.checkboxGroupParams.placement. Stores the selected values as an array.
when_to_use: When the viewSpec.type is 'checkbox_group' for an array field with an enum
When users need to select multiple values from a fixed list using checkboxes
avoid: Do not use when single-select from an enum is needed — use RadioGroup or Select instead
Do not use when no enum is defined on the spec

name: DateInput
description: A string input component that integrates GravityUI DatePicker for date/time selection. Supports multiple output formats: ISO string, custom format string, 'date_time', 'date', or 'timestamp' (seconds/nanos). Reads timezone and format from viewSpec.dateInput.
when_to_use: When the viewSpec.type is 'date_input' for a string field
When the field stores a date or datetime as a string or timestamp object
avoid: Do not use for plain text fields — use Text instead
Avoid when the field type is not string

name: DynamicField
description: The main entry point for rendering a dynamic form field backed by React Final Form. Accepts a field name, a Spec describing the field schema, and a DynamicFormConfig mapping spec types to input and layout components. Provides context for config, Monaco editor, search, shared state, and mutators to all child components.
when_to_use: When embedding a JSON-schema-driven form field inside a React Final Form
When rendering any dynamic form field specified by a Spec
avoid: Do not use outside of a React Final Form context
Do not use when a read-only value display is needed — use DynamicView instead

props.name: name
props.description: Final Form field name used to bind the field to form state.

props.name: spec
props.description: JSON schema spec describing the field type, validation rules, and view configuration.

props.name: config
props.description: Configuration object mapping spec types to input and layout components.

props.name: destroyOnUnregister
props.description: Whether to destroy the field value when the component unmounts; defaults to true.

props.name: generateRandomValue
props.description: Function that generates a random value for string fields with the generate button enabled.

props.name: Monaco
props.description: Monaco editor component to use for monaco_input fields.

props.name: mutators
props.description: External mutator functions injected into the form context.

props.name: search
props.type: string | ((spec: Spec, input: FieldValue, name: string) => boolean)
props.description: Search string or function for filtering visible fields.

props.name: shared
props.description: Shared state object available to all child components via context.

props.name: storeSubscriber
props.description: Callback that receives the current field store on every change.

props.name: withoutInsertFFDebounce
props.description: Disables debouncing when syncing values into React Final Form.

name: DynamicView
description: The main entry point for rendering a read-only display of form values driven by a Spec and a DynamicViewConfig. Mirrors the structure of DynamicField but shows values rather than editable inputs. Supports an optional custom Link component for link-type fields.
when_to_use: When displaying a read-only overview of values that were collected with DynamicField
When a value preview driven by a Spec is needed without any editing capability
avoid: Do not use when the user needs to edit the values — use DynamicField instead

props.name: config
props.description: Configuration object mapping spec types to view and layout components.

props.name: spec
props.description: JSON schema spec describing the structure and view configuration.

props.name: value
props.description: The value to display.

props.name: Link
props.description: Custom component for rendering link-type fields.

props.name: Monaco
props.description: Monaco editor component for monaco_input view fields.

props.name: shared
props.description: Shared state object available to all child view components.

props.name: showLayoutDescription
props.description: When true layout descriptions are shown in view layouts.

name: FileInput
description: A string input component that renders a file upload button. Reads the selected file using a FileReader with a configurable readAsMethod and stores the result as a string value. Supports accept filter and an inline reset button.
when_to_use: When the viewSpec.type is 'file_input' for a string field
When the user needs to upload a file whose contents are stored as a string in the form
avoid: Do not use for plain text entry — use Text or TextArea instead

name: Group
description: A layout component derived from Section that renders a titled section with left-side indentation via GroupIndent. Exported in two size variants: Group (medium title) and Group2 (small title). Used as the 'group' and 'group2' layout types in dynamicConfig.
when_to_use: When the viewSpec.layout is 'group' or 'group2' for any field type
When a titled section with left indentation of child fields is needed
avoid: Do not use when indentation is not wanted — use Section or Row instead

name: GroupIndent
description: A simple layout utility component that adds a left-side visual indentation to its children. Used internally by Group and OneOf to visually nest sub-fields under a parent.
when_to_use: When sub-fields should be visually indented under a parent field or section
avoid: Avoid using directly when a full section or group layout is appropriate

name: MonacoInput
description: A string input component that embeds a Monaco code editor with an optional dialog expand button. Reads language, fontSize, and other display options from viewSpec.monacoParams. Requires a Monaco editor component to be passed either via the DynamicField Monaco prop or the component's own MonacoComponent prop.
when_to_use: When the viewSpec.type is 'monaco_input' for a string field
When a code or structured text editor with syntax highlighting is needed
avoid: Do not use without providing a Monaco editor component — renders nothing if missing
Do not use for plain single-line text — use Text instead

name: MultiOneOf
description: An object input that renders a multi-select dropdown to choose multiple active sub-properties simultaneously. Each selected property renders its Controller inline below the selector. Supports optional GroupIndent via the withoutIndent prop. The flat variant MultiOneOfFlat removes the indentation.
when_to_use: When the viewSpec.type is 'multi_oneof' or 'multi_oneof_flat' for an object field
When multiple sub-properties of an object can be enabled simultaneously via a multi-select
avoid: Do not use when only one sub-property can be active at a time — use OneOf instead

name: MultiSelect
description: An array input component that renders a multi-select dropdown populated from the spec's enum values. Converts between the internal array format and string arrays using transformArrIn/transformArrOut helpers. Used as the 'select' array input type in dynamicConfig.
when_to_use: When the viewSpec.type is 'select' for an array field with an enum
When users need to select multiple values from a dropdown
avoid: Do not use for single-select — use Select instead
Do not use when checkbox-style multi-select is preferred — use CheckboxGroup instead

name: NumberWithScale
description: A string input component that pairs a text input with a scale unit selector (e.g. bytes/KB/MB). Stores the value in the base unit defined by defaultType in viewSpec.sizeParams. Automatically converts the displayed value when the scale dropdown changes.
when_to_use: When the viewSpec.type is 'number_with_scale' for a string field
When the field stores a numeric value in a base unit but the user should be able to view and enter it in scaled units
avoid: Do not use when no sizeParams are defined in the spec — the component returns null
Do not use for plain number input without a scale — use NumInput instead

name: NumInput
description: A number input component that wraps GravityUI NumberInput. Registered as the 'base' number input type in dynamicConfig. Handles null-to-undefined conversion and passes min/max constraints from the spec.
when_to_use: When the viewSpec.type is 'base' for a number field
When a standard numeric input is needed
avoid: Do not use for numbers stored as strings with a scale unit — use NumberWithScale instead

name: ObjectBase
description: An object input component that iterates over the spec's properties and renders a Controller for each one. Supports an optional inline rendering mode via ObjectInline for side-by-side fields. Respects viewSpec.order and viewSpec.delimiter for layout control. Shows an add button when the object value is undefined.
when_to_use: When the viewSpec.type is 'base' or 'inline' for an object field
When a form section needs to render all properties of an ObjectSpec
avoid: Do not use when a oneOf/multi-oneof selection pattern is needed — use OneOf or MultiOneOf instead

name: OneOf
description: An object input component that shows a toggler (radio group, select, checkbox, switch, or card) and renders the sub-spec of only the selected property. The toggler type is chosen automatically based on the number of options and viewSpec.oneOfParams.toggler. The flat variant OneOfFlat removes the GroupIndent wrapper.
when_to_use: When the viewSpec.type is 'oneof' or 'oneof_flat' for an object field
When only one sub-property of an object should be active at a time
avoid: Do not use when multiple sub-properties can be active simultaneously — use MultiOneOf instead
Do not use when a card-based toggler layout is needed — use CardOneOf instead

name: RadioGroup
description: A string input component that renders a GravityUI RadioGroup from the spec's enum values. Supports horizontal or vertical direction via viewSpec.radioGroupParams.direction and per-option disabled state. Used as the 'radio_group' string input type in dynamicConfig.
when_to_use: When the viewSpec.type is 'radio_group' for a string field with an enum
When a small set of mutually exclusive options should be shown as radio buttons
avoid: Do not use for large enums — use Select instead for better UX
Do not use for multi-select — use CheckboxGroup or MultiSelect instead

name: Row
description: A horizontal layout component that places the field label on the left and the input on the right. Displays a HelpMark tooltip for layoutDescription. Includes an inline remove button for array items and a GenerateRandomValueButton for string fields that have it enabled. RowVerbose variant shows the description below the input instead of in a tooltip.
when_to_use: When the viewSpec.layout is 'row' or 'row_verbose' for any field type
When a classic label-left input-right form row layout is needed
avoid: Do not use when a vertical section layout is preferred — use Section instead

name: Secret
description: An object input component designed for secret/credential fields. It expects the object spec to have a 'raw' property and renders only that sub-field with its layout forced to 'transparent'. Used as the 'secret' object input type in dynamicConfig.
when_to_use: When the viewSpec.type is 'secret' for an object field that wraps a sensitive value under a 'raw' property
avoid: Do not use for a plain password text field — use Text with viewSpec.type='password' instead
Do not use when there is no 'raw' property in the object spec

name: Section
description: A vertical layout component that renders a titled section header above the field content. Available in multiple variants: Section (medium title), Section2 (small title), SectionWithSubtitle (description as subtitle), SectionWithSubtitle2, Group (with GroupIndent), Group2. Wraps children in ErrorWrapper for form validation feedback.
when_to_use: When the viewSpec.layout is 'section', 'section2', 'group', or 'group2'
When a field or group of fields needs a visible title header above the inputs
avoid: Do not use when a side-by-side label-input layout is needed — use Row instead
import_statement: import { Section, Section2, SectionWithSubtitle, SectionWithSubtitle2, Group, Group2 } from '@gravity-ui/dynamic-forms'

name: Select
description: A string input component that renders a GravityUI Select populated from the spec's enum values. Automatically enables filtering when there are more than 9 options. Supports custom options and meta text for each option via selectParams. Used as the 'select' string input type in dynamicConfig.
when_to_use: When the viewSpec.type is 'select' for a string field with an enum
When a single-value selection from a list is needed
avoid: Do not use for multi-select — use MultiSelect instead
Do not use for radio button style — use RadioGroup for small enums instead

name: Switch
description: A boolean input component that wraps GravityUI Switch. Registered as the 'switch' boolean input type in dynamicConfig. Reads and writes boolean values via the dynamic-forms field integration.
when_to_use: When the viewSpec.type is 'switch' for a boolean field
When a toggle switch UI is preferred over a checkbox for a boolean value
avoid: Do not use for checkbox-style UI — use Checkbox instead

name: TableArrayInput
description: An array input component that renders items in a GravityUI Table. Each row corresponds to an array item and each column corresponds to an object property defined in viewSpec.table. Requires items to be an ObjectSpec and table column definitions to be provided. Supports column widths and description tooltips per column.
when_to_use: When the viewSpec.type is 'table' for an array field whose items are objects
When array data should be displayed and edited in a tabular format
avoid: Do not use when items are not objects — use ArrayBase instead
Do not use without viewSpec.table column definitions — the component returns null

name: Text
description: A string (or number) input component that renders a GravityUI TextInput or PasswordInput. Used as the 'base' and 'password' string input types in dynamicConfig. Shows a PasswordInput when viewSpec.type is 'password'.
when_to_use: When the viewSpec.type is 'base' for a string or number field
When the viewSpec.type is 'password' for a masked text input
avoid: Do not use for multi-line text — use TextArea instead
Do not use for code editing — use MonacoInput instead

name: TextArea
description: A string input component that renders a GravityUI TextArea with 8–20 rows. Used as the 'textarea' string input type in dynamicConfig. Passes through additional TextArea props via inputProps.
when_to_use: When the viewSpec.type is 'textarea' for a string field
When the user needs to enter multi-line text
avoid: Do not use for single-line text — use Text instead
Do not use for code — use MonacoInput instead

name: TextContent
description: A string independent input that renders static display content derived from viewSpec.textContentParams or viewSpec.layoutDescription. Can render as an Alert, a Label, or plain text with an optional icon. Used as the 'text_content' string input type in dynamicConfig.
when_to_use: When the viewSpec.type is 'text_content' for a string field
When a static informational text, alert, or label should be embedded in the form
avoid: Do not use for editable text — use Text or TextArea instead

name: Transparent
description: A pass-through layout component that renders children without any visual wrapper. Used as the 'transparent' layout type in dynamicConfig to suppress any layout chrome around a field.
when_to_use: When the viewSpec.layout is 'transparent'
When a field should render without any label, title, or container
avoid: Do not use when a label or title is needed — use Row or Section instead

name: useErrorChecker
description: A hook that automatically opens a collapsible container when the form is submitted and a validation error exists inside it. Accepts the field name, meta object, current open state, and a setOpen callback.
when_to_use: When building a collapsible container that should auto-expand on submit errors for fields inside it
avoid: Do not use outside of a dynamic-forms form context — it depends on Final Form meta
signature: useErrorChecker({ name: string, meta: FieldRenderProps<FieldValue>['meta'], open: boolean, setOpen: (flag: boolean) => void }): void

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: The field name to look for in form error elements.

parameters.name: meta
parameters.description: Final Form meta object providing submitFailed state.

parameters.name: open
parameters.description: Current open state of the collapsible container.

parameters.name: setOpen
parameters.description: Callback to change the open state.

name: useOneOf
description: A hook that manages the toggler logic for oneOf-style object inputs. Determines the toggler type (radio, select, card, checkbox, or switch) based on the number of options and viewSpec.oneOfParams. Returns the current selected key, spec properties map, the rendered toggler element, and the raw toggler input.
when_to_use: When building a custom oneOf input component that needs the toggler logic
When the built-in OneOf, CardOneOf patterns need to be extended
avoid: Do not use for multi-select scenarios — this hook manages single active property selection
signature: useOneOf({ props: ObjectIndependentInputProps, onTogglerChange?: (value: string) => void }): { oneOfValue: string, specProperties: Record<string, Spec>, toggler: React.ReactNode, togglerInput: React.ReactNode }

parameters.name: props
parameters.description: The full props of the parent object input component.

parameters.name: onTogglerChange
parameters.description: Optional callback fired when the selected property changes.

return_type: { oneOfValue: string, specProperties: Record<string, Spec>, toggler: React.ReactNode, togglerInput: React.ReactNode }

name: dynamicConfig
description: The default DynamicFormConfig object that maps all built-in input, layout, and validator components to their spec type and viewSpec.type keys. Pass this to DynamicField as the config prop to use all standard form components out of the box.
when_to_use: When setting up DynamicField with the default set of input and layout components
As the starting point to extend with custom inputs or layouts
avoid: Do not pass to DynamicView — use dynamicViewConfig instead

name: dynamicViewConfig
description: The default DynamicViewConfig object that maps all built-in view and view-layout components to their spec type and viewSpec.type keys. Pass this to DynamicView as the config prop to use all standard view components out of the box.
when_to_use: When setting up DynamicView with the default set of view and layout components
avoid: Do not pass to DynamicField — use dynamicConfig instead

name: getArrayValidator
description: A factory function that returns a validator for ArraySpec fields. Validates required, maxLength, and minLength constraints. Accepts optional ignore flags and customErrorMessages to override default error strings.
when_to_use: When registering a custom array validator in a DynamicFormConfig
When customizing the built-in array validation rules
avoid: Do not use for non-array spec types — use the corresponding getXxxValidator instead
signature: getArrayValidator(params?: GetArrayValidatorParams): (spec: ArraySpec, value?: ArrayValue) => string | false

parameters.name: params
parameters.description: Optional flags to skip specific checks and custom error messages.

name: getBooleanValidator
description: A factory function that returns a validator for BooleanSpec fields. Validates the required constraint. Accepts optional ignoreRequiredCheck and customErrorMessages.
when_to_use: When registering a custom boolean validator in a DynamicFormConfig
signature: getBooleanValidator(params?: GetBooleanValidatorParams): (spec: BooleanSpec, value?: boolean) => string | false

parameters.name: params
parameters.description: Optional flags and custom error messages.

name: getNumberValidator
description: A factory function that returns a validator for NumberSpec fields. Validates required, maximum, minimum, int64 format, leading/trailing spaces, zero-start, and other numeric format rules. Each check can be individually disabled via ignore flags.
when_to_use: When registering a custom number validator in a DynamicFormConfig
When customizing numeric validation rules such as disabling integer-only check
signature: getNumberValidator(params?: GetNumberValidatorParams): (spec: NumberSpec, value?: string | number) => string | false

parameters.name: params
parameters.description: Optional ignore flags for each numeric check and custom error messages.

name: getObjectValidator
description: A factory function that returns a validator for ObjectSpec fields. Validates the required constraint. Accepts optional ignoreRequiredCheck and customErrorMessages.
when_to_use: When registering a custom object validator in a DynamicFormConfig
signature: getObjectValidator(params?: GetObjectValidatorParams): (spec: ObjectSpec, value?: ObjectValue) => string | false

parameters.name: params
parameters.description: Optional flags and custom error messages.

name: getStringValidator
description: A factory function that returns a validator for StringSpec fields. Validates required, maxLength, minLength, pattern (regex), and leading/trailing space rules. Each check can be individually disabled via ignore flags.
when_to_use: When registering a custom string validator in a DynamicFormConfig
When customizing string validation rules such as disabling the pattern check
signature: getStringValidator(params?: GetStringValidatorParams): (spec: StringSpec, value?: string) => string | false

parameters.name: params
parameters.description: Optional ignore flags for each string check and custom error messages.
